{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Automation Engineer",
  "description": "Seven-Up Bottling Company Limited is one of the largest manufacturing companies in Nigeria, producing and distributing some of the nation&rsquo;s most- loved beverages in the country like; Pepsi, 7Up, Mirinda, Teem, Mountain Dew, H2oH!, Lipton Ice Tea and Aquafina premium drinking water. SBC has recently entered into Home &amp; Personal Care category with the 2SURE brand and launched its first product, a Hand &amp; Surface Sanitizer. Seven Up boasts of nine bottling plants with state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities strategically located across various regions in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Automation Engineer

Job Location: Nigeria

Job Summary


	Provide and ensure leadership in the design, development and deployment of reliable and sustainable electrical and automation systems of all bottling and utilities equipment in the plant.


Responsibilities


	Provide quality and timely support to the plant in the events of complex automation and electrical challenges.
	Set some level of standards in the plant automation operating procedures.
	Ensure basic and standard automation tools are available for the automation team of the plant.
	Facilitate the use of condition monitoring tools for the routine assessment of automation equipment status in the plant.
	Ensure availability and regular update of all user program backup for all automation equipment in the plant.
	Ensure availability and regular update of electrical documentation for all equipment in the plant.
	Conduct routine audit of all electrical controls of all equipment in the plant.
	Develop a list of critical automation spare parts that will be available to prevent stoppages of the production lines/utility.
	Regularly communicate to management any critical actions needed to restore the condition of the equipment to 100% reliability.
	Facilitate the capability development of the plant automation team to keep with current trends.
	Prepare annual automation engineering budget &ndash; CAPEX and R&amp;M (zero based budgets).
	Ensures implementation of basic cycle maintenance, cleaning and inspection and instill these principles to be part of the culture of operators and technicians.
	Take lead in change management for the engineering/automation department related to people structure or role changes, technology enhancement, regulatory and company initiatives on productivity.


Requirements


	A minimum of Bachelor of Engineering Degree (B.Eng.) or HND in Mechanical/Electrical Engineering.
	5&ndash; 7 years of relevant hands-on FMCG operation and maintenance experience.
	At least 2 year of&nbsp; experience in a beverage bottling company in a managerial capacity.
	Experienced in budgeting and people management
	Knowledge of bottling operations: Planning, Manufacturing, Quality, Environment, Engineering, Health and Safety
	Operational planning expertise/experience
	Understanding of financial impact of decisions
	Coaching direct reports and mentoring others


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@sevenup.org using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-08-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {

  },

    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",

    "value": {

      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }

}



Automation Engineer Job at Seven-Up Bottling Company Limited




        
    

        
        Seven-Up Bottling Company Limited is one of the largest manufacturing companies in Nigeria, producing and distributing some of the nation’s most- loved beverages in the country like; Pepsi, 7Up, Mirinda, Teem, Mountain Dew, H2oH!, Lipton Ice Tea and Aquafina premium drinking water. SBC has recently entered into Home & Personal Care category with the 2SURE brand and launched its first product, a Hand & Surface Sanitizer. Seven Up boasts of nine bottling plants with state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities strategically located across various regions in the country.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Automation Engineer

Job Location: Nigeria
     

            
    



Job Summary


	Provide and ensure leadership in the design, development and deployment of reliable and sustainable electrical and automation systems of all bottling and utilities equipment in the plant.


Responsibilities


	Provide quality and timely support to the plant in the events of complex automation and electrical challenges.
	Set some level of standards in the plant automation operating procedures.
	Ensure basic and standard automation tools are available for the automation team of the plant.
	Facilitate the use of condition monitoring tools for the routine assessment of automation equipment status in the plant.
	Ensure availability and regular update of all user program backup for all automation equipment in the plant.
	Ensure availability and regular update of electrical documentation for all equipment in the plant.
	Conduct routine audit of all electrical controls of all equipment in the plant.
	Develop a list of critical automation spare parts that will be available to prevent stoppages of the production lines/utility.
	Regularly communicate to management any critical actions needed to restore the condition of the equipment to 100% reliability.
	Facilitate the capability development of the plant automation team to keep with current trends.
	Prepare annual automation engineering budget – CAPEX and R&M (zero based budgets).
	Ensures implementation of basic cycle maintenance, cleaning and inspection and instill these principles to be part of the culture of operators and technicians.
	Take lead in change management for the engineering/automation department related to people structure or role changes, technology enhancement, regulatory and company initiatives on productivity.


Requirements


	A minimum of Bachelor of Engineering Degree (B.Eng.) or HND in Mechanical/Electrical Engineering.
	5– 7 years of relevant hands-on FMCG operation and maintenance experience.
	At least 2 year of  experience in a beverage bottling company in a managerial capacity.
	Experienced in budgeting and people management
	Knowledge of bottling operations: Planning, Manufacturing, Quality, Environment, Engineering, Health and Safety
	Operational planning expertise/experience
	Understanding of financial impact of decisions
	Coaching direct reports and mentoring others


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Manager, Specialised Sectors",
  "description": "Stanbic IBTC Bank is a leading African banking group focused on emerging markets globally. It has been a mainstay of South Africa&#39;s financial system for 150 years, and now spans 16 countries across the African continent.

Standard Bank is a firm believer in technical innovation, to help us guarantee exceptional client service and leading edge financial solutions. Our growing global success reflects our commitment to the latest solutions, the best people, and a uniquely flexible and vibrant working culture. To help us drive our success into the future, we are looking for resourceful individuals to join our dedicated team at our offices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Manager, Specialised Sectors

Job Location: Abuja
Job Type: Full-time
Business Segment: Business &amp; Commercial Banking

Job Description


	To take a strategic approach to develop business opportunities within the Specialised Sectors, North portfolio.
	The Manager, Specialised Sectors North will map out opportunities, formulate strategies and track strategy execution in the North.
	This role requires gravitas and adaptability to be able to engage at all levels within organizations from the executive leadership level to the lowest level.


Qualifications


	First Degree in any field.
	5-10 years of experience in the banking industry.
	Prior experience in business development, sales and/or operations and a track record of achieving sales targets.
	Experience within the financial industry with experience in managing small businesses.


Additional Information:
Behavioural Competencies:


	Documenting Facts.
	Establishing Rapport.
	Generating Ideas.
	Examining Information.
	Team Working.


Technical Competencies:


	Business Analysis Planning &amp; Monitoring.
	Requirements Elicitation.
	Requirements Management &amp; Communication.
	Requirements Analysis.
	Solution Assessment &amp; Validation.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-23",

Manager, Specialised Sectors Job at Stanbic IBTC Bank




        
    

        
        Stanbic IBTC Bank is a leading African banking group focused on emerging markets globally. It has been a mainstay of South Africa's financial system for 150 years, and now spans 16 countries across the African continent.

Standard Bank is a firm believer in technical innovation, to help us guarantee exceptional client service and leading edge financial solutions. Our growing global success reflects our commitment to the latest solutions, the best people, and a uniquely flexible and vibrant working culture. To help us drive our success into the future, we are looking for resourceful individuals to join our dedicated team at our offices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Manager, Specialised Sectors
     

            
    



Job Location: Abuja
Job Type: Full-time
Business Segment: Business & Commercial Banking

Job Description


	To take a strategic approach to develop business opportunities within the Specialised Sectors, North portfolio.
	The Manager, Specialised Sectors North will map out opportunities, formulate strategies and track strategy execution in the North.
	This role requires gravitas and adaptability to be able to engage at all levels within organizations from the executive leadership level to the lowest level.


Qualifications


	First Degree in any field.
	5-10 years of experience in the banking industry.
	Prior experience in business development, sales and/or operations and a track record of achieving sales targets.
	Experience within the financial industry with experience in managing small businesses.


Additional Information:
Behavioural Competencies:


	Documenting Facts.
	Establishing Rapport.
	Generating Ideas.
	Examining Information.
	Team Working.


Technical Competencies:


	Business Analysis Planning & Monitoring.
	Requirements Elicitation.
	Requirements Management & Communication.
	Requirements Analysis.
	Solution Assessment & Validation.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Location: Lagos

Job brief


	We are looking for a competent Inventory Manager to monitor and report on the company&rsquo;s inventory. Your job is important since the efficient handling of the company&rsquo;s products and supplies is critical for the attainment of business goals.
	A successful inventory manager is a reliable professional with excellent record-keeping abilities. They possess great attention to detail and a business mindset.
	The goal is to ensure that all business operations have adequate material to achieve their objectives.


Responsibilities


	Devise ways to optimize inventory control procedures
	Inspect the levels of business supplies and raw material to identify shortages
	Ensure product stock is adequate for all distribution channels and can cover direct demand from customers
	Record daily deliveries and shipments to reconcile inventory
	Use software to monitor demand and document characteristics of inventory
	Place orders to replenish stock avoiding insufficiencies or excessive surplus
	Analyze data to anticipate future needs
	Evaluate suppliers to achieve cost-effective deals and maintain trust relationships
	Collaborate with warehouse employees and other staff to ensure business goals are met
	Report to upper management on stock levels, issues etc.

	Relevant qualifications (e.g. CPIM) will be a plus
	Proven experience as inventory manager or similar position
	Excellent knowledge of data analysis and forecasting methods
	Working knowledge of inventory management software (e.g. ERP)
	Ability to accurately track inventory and create reports
	An analytical mind with strong math skills
	Excellent organizational and planning skills
	Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities
	Reliable and trustworthy.

N120,000 - N180,000 per month.&nbsp;

Inventory Controller Job at Elite Logistics & Development Services Limited




        
    

        
        Elite Logistics & Development Services Limited is an indigenous company with its operating office in Lagos and duly registered under the companies and allied matters ACT 1990 with RC1195367. ELADS Limited prides itself in meeting modern-day civil engineering, construction and general contracts administration challenges and ensuring timely, successful completion of projects.
     

            
    



Elite Logistics & Development Services Limited strives to conduct its business fairly and honestly with consideration to the long-term needs of all its stakeholders. Competitive tendering, professional management, quality construction, an excellent safety record, a proven ability, cooperative and proactive approach have ensured our delivery of highest quality workman Poship and client satisfaction in both the public and private sectonrs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Controller
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job brief


	We are looking for a competent Inventory Manager to monitor and report on the company’s inventory. Your job is important since the efficient handling of the company’s products and supplies is critical for the attainment of business goals.
	A successful inventory manager is a reliable professional with excellent record-keeping abilities. They possess great attention to detail and a business mindset.
	The goal is to ensure that all business operations have adequate material to achieve their objectives.


Responsibilities


	Devise ways to optimize inventory control procedures
	Inspect the levels of business supplies and raw material to identify shortages
	Ensure product stock is adequate for all distribution channels and can cover direct demand from customers
	Record daily deliveries and shipments to reconcile inventory
	Use software to monitor demand and document characteristics of inventory
	Place orders to replenish stock avoiding insufficiencies or excessive surplus
	Analyze data to anticipate future needs
	Evaluate suppliers to achieve cost-effective deals and maintain trust relationships
	Collaborate with warehouse employees and other staff to ensure business goals are met
	Report to upper management on stock levels, issues etc.


Requirements and skills


	BSc / BA in Business Administration, Logistics or relevant field;
	Relevant qualifications (e.g. CPIM) will be a plus
	Proven experience as inventory manager or similar position
	Excellent knowledge of data analysis and forecasting methods
	Working knowledge of inventory management software (e.g. ERP)
	Ability to accurately track inventory and create reports
	An analytical mind with strong math skills
	Excellent organizational and planning skills
	Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities
	Reliable and trustworthy.


Salary
N120,000 - N180,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

title: Job Openings at Botare Creation Nigeria Limited
content:
Botare Creation Nigeria Limited is an international company specialized in the production and distribution of high-quality tissue paper and home care products. We are committed to providing affordable, hygienic, and reliable household solutions that meet the everyday needs of consumers.

We continue to expand our presence across global markets through efficient operations, modern manufacturing standards, and strategic partnerships. Driven by excellence and continuous improvement, Botare Creation aims to become a trusted household brand known for quality, convenience, and value.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Merchandiser / Promoter
     

            
    



Job Locations: Ogun & Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Merchandiser/Promoter will beresponsible for ensuring the effective display, availability, and promotion of company products in retail outlets to drive sales, enhance brand visibility, and achieve business objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Ensure products are properly displayed, priced, labeled, and stocked in line with company standards.
	Monitor stock levels, replenish products, and remove expired or damaged items from shelves.
	Promote products to customers, conduct demonstrations where necessary, and drive sales to achieve assigned targets.
	Implement promotional activities and maintain attractive product displays using point-of-sale materials.
	Build and maintain good relationships with customers, store personnel, and other stakeholders.
	Gather customer feedback, monitor competitor activities, and provide market insights to management.
	Maintain accurate records of sales, stock movements, and daily activities, and submit reports as required.
	Comply with company policies and participate in training, meetings, and other assigned activities.


Key Skills and Requirements


	Previous experience as a merchandiser/promoter is an added advantage.
	Strong communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills.
	Good sales and negotiation abilities.
	Attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
	Ability to work independently, manage time effectively, and meet targets.
	Basic reporting and record-keeping skills.


Salary
N120,000 - N200,000 per month.

Application Closing Date: 4th August, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

2.)  Sales Supervisor (Tissue & Home care Products)

Job Location: Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Develop and execute sales strategies to achieve revenue and market share targets.
	Identify, onboard, and manage distributors and key partners
	Drive customer acquisition and retention across assigned territories
	Monitor market trends and competitor activities, and provide actionable insights.
	Ensure consistent market visits, field execution, and reporting.
	Manage and support field sales teams where applicable.
	Track performance against KPIs and implement corrective actions where necessary.
	Ensure product availability, pricing compliance, and visibility across assigned territories.
	Review stock levels and demand forecasts and support distributors with stock planning
	Handle escalations from key retailers and trade partners.
	Oversee trade activations, marketing campaigns, and product launches in the field.
	Prepare weekly/monthly sales performance reports and recommend strategic actions.


Requirements


	OND / Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or related field
	Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in FMCG sales or a related industry with at least 1–2 years in field supervision.
	Proven experience in distribution and market expansion
	Good knowledge of the Ibadan markets
	Strong network of distributors, wholesalers, and retailers.
	Excellent leadership, communication, and negotiation skills.


Salary
N600,000 - N900,000 per month. 

Application Closing Date: 30th June, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

 

3.)  Marketing Supervisor

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	The Marketing Supervisor oversees the execution of marketing campaigns, brand visibility initiatives, and promotional activities, while translating high-level strategies into actionable daily tasks and ensuring projects are delivered on time, within budget, and with maximum impact


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate marketing campaigns, events, activations, and promotional activities.
	Supervise content creation and manage the company’s digital and social media platforms.
	Monitor campaign performance, digital metrics, and prepare marketing reports.
	Conduct market research and competitor analysis to support business growth.
	Supervise junior marketing staff, assign tasks, and monitor team performance.
	Coordinate with vendors, agencies, and internal departments for smooth project execution.
	Support budget monitoring and ensure cost-effective marketing operations.
	Collaborate with Sales and Operations teams to align marketing activities with business objectives.


Job Requirements


	B.Sc/ HND in Marketing, Mass Communication, Business Administration, or related field.
	3–4 years of marketing experience with at least 1 year in a supervisory role.
	Strong project execution skills.
	Good communication, leadership, and vendor management skills.
	Basic knowledge of digital marketing analytics and campaign tracking.
	Creative thinking and understanding of content and branding principles.


Salary
N600,000 - N900,000 per month.

Application Closing Date: 30th June, 2026.

Click here to apply online

     

    

        Click here to Apply
